summary: History-adjusted school closure estimate that compares forecast snow, cold, and wind against the last five years of nearby winter weather, then combines that with estimated ice, storm timing, and user-selected school policy signals.

weights.label: Snow
weights.method: Forecast snowfall is normalized against nearby historical snowfall totals from the seasonal window around the forecast date.

weights.label: Ice
weights.method: Ice remains a direct risk factor, but the fetched value is a capped estimate built from freezing-weather signals because reliable local ice-history coverage is less consistent.

weights.label: Storm Timing
weights.method: Timing is a heuristic operational signal. Morning commute and overnight storms score higher than after-school events.

weights.label: Temperature
weights.method: Colder-than-usual mornings receive higher scores by comparing forecast temperature to nearby historical winter lows.

weights.label: Wind
weights.method: Wind is normalized against nearby historical winter maximum wind speeds for the same seasonal window.

weights.label: School Policy
weights.method: School type and district strictness remain user-supplied heuristic policy inputs because district-level operational rules are not publicly standardized.

data_sources.name: NOAA Climate Data Online
data_sources.role: Public climate reference and methodology support

limitations: This model is not an official school district closure formula.
The tool does not yet ingest full district-by-district historical closure feeds nationwide.
Estimated ice is inferred from forecast freezing-weather signals, not direct measured ice accumulation.
School type and district strictness are user-entered policy signals, not official district operations data.
Location-based weather lookups work best where forecast and archive coverage are both available.
next_steps: Expand the hand-audited closure dataset into a larger benchmark set.
Backtest the model against more public district notices across multiple winters.
Move validation scoring into a reproducible backend job once a larger closure-history corpus is available.
